subject: Computer Networking
title: The OSI Model
description: A 60-second lesson on The OSI Model.

hook.text: Networking was chaotic in the 1970s. The OSI model tried to organize it into seven clean layers.

buildup.text: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application — each layer has a clear job.

discovery.text: The model helps engineers discuss networking without confusion. Layer 3 means routing. Layer 4 means transport.

twist.text: The OSI protocols themselves never caught on. TCP/IP won in practice. But the model remained as a teaching tool.

climax.text: Every networking textbook, certification, and interview references the OSI model. It's the shared vocabulary.

punchline.text: OSI lost the protocol war but won the education one.

quiz.question: How many layers does the OSI model define?
